single.brief_rationale: Single agent successfully completed the task with top 10 error days, three archetypes, and remediations. Used a simple historical average model rather than proper ML. Had encoding issues that required iteration. Archetypes show some overlap (weekend appears in multiple categories). Visualizations were generated but methodology lacked formal validation steps.

multi.brief_rationale: Multi-agent run demonstrates explicit staged workflow (Planner→ResearchAgent→DataScientist→CodeReviewerAgent) with visible review loops. CodeReviewerAgent provided blocking fixes that were addressed, including ensuring three DISTINCT archetypes with unique names and archetype-specific remediations. Used K-Means clustering for data-driven archetype formation. Generated 4 comprehensive visualizations (bar chart, heatmap, boxplots, PCA). Minor Unicode error at end did not affect deliverables.

scores.comparison_notes: Multi-agent run wins due to stronger workflow evidence and contract compliance per the complexity-bias policy. The transcript shows explicit planning, research recommendations, code reviewer blocking fixes, and validation that three distinct archetypes were created. The CodeReviewerAgent caught the duplicate archetype naming issue and forced a fix, demonstrating the review-gated QA loop. Single agent completed the task but lacked the staged validation and had overlapping archetype definitions. Multi-agent's K-Means clustering approach was more methodologically sound than single agent's heuristic grouping.
